coverage.evidence: Digits and CIFAR grid metrics plus merge landscape figures.
results/digits_merge/figures/per_task_basin_overlay.png.
Digits, CIFAR, and Qwen path/lambda sweeps.
Digits method table and overlay cover average, task arithmetic, SLERP, TIES, DARE, TIES+DARE, Fisher, RegMean, layer-wise task arithmetic, and validation grid search.
Digits, CIFAR, and pairwise single-digit conflict tables/figures.
Ten single-digit experts and all 45 pairwise merges.
Independent-initialization MLP path before/after Hungarian hidden-unit alignment.
CIFAR-10 vehicle/animal GroupNorm CNN merge landscape.
CIFAR100 ViT-style from-scratch transformer and ImageNet-pretrained ViT-B/16 frozen-backbone transfer merge studies are present.
Safetensors probe and same-file smoke test.
Qwen2.5-1.5B base-to-instruct path is evaluated with fixed NLL prompts plus GSM8K, MMLU, and HumanEval benchmark slices.
Qwen2.5-0.5B base, Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct, and Qwen2.5-Coder-0.5B-Instruct are evaluated in a two-expert merge plane.
Representative Qwen2.5-1.5B benchmark slices cover MMLU, GSM8K, HumanEval canonical-solution NLL, and BeaverTails safety/refusal NLL.

scripts/run_vllm_downstream_eval.py can build a served-model eval plan from the Qwen target registry; the generic registry run remains endpoint_unavailable, while checkpoint-specific hosted eval is tracked separately.

results/vllm_checkpoint_eval/qwen_0_5b_instruct_coder_uniform_average/report.md contains a real vLLM-hosted GSM8K/MMLU/safety/HumanEval compile eval for the materialized Qwen2.5-0.5B uniform-average checkpoint.
results/vllm_source_merge_comparison/report.md compares Qwen2.5-0.5B base/instruct/coder source endpoints against the materialized uniform-average checkpoint under the same vLLM downstream tasks.

results/probe_guided_dense_average_candidate/report.md selects a non-uniform Qwen instruct/coder bridge from the NLL grid, materializes the same-shape checkpoint locally, and records its real vLLM downstream eval.
coverage.item: Probe-guided dense average candidate vLLM eval

coverage.evidence: results/qwen_dense_module_guarded_candidate/report.md, results/qwen_dense_norm_guarded_candidate/report.md, and results/qwen_dense_selective_norm_guarded_candidate/report.md compare module-level, norm-only, and selective-norm tensor-rule variants against the global bridge under the same vLLM downstream tasks.
coverage.item: Qwen dense module-wise guard ablation vLLM eval

coverage.evidence: results/vllm_downstream_eval_smoke/smoke_report.md validates the OpenAI-compatible HTTP request, answer parsing, scoring, model ranking, and artifact writing path using a local mock endpoint.

results/vllm_checkpoint_eval_plan/report.md turns same-shape checkpoint candidates into one-checkpoint-at-a-time vLLM serve/eval commands while keeping missing checkpoints separate from completed metrics.
results/checkpoint_materialization_readiness/report.md audits writer commands, placeholders, dry-run outputs, checkpoint existence, and vLLM eval readiness in one table.
results/average_decision_report/report.md converts merge grids, conflict probes, and optional MoE routing probes into same-shape average decisions.
results/model_averaging_literature_review/report.md maps recent model averaging and MoE merging papers to probes, failure signals, and same-shape writer actions.
results/qwen_target_model_registry/report.md maps representative official, third-party, downstream, and adapter-pool Qwen candidates to scenarios, eval slices, probes, and same-shape topology gates.
results/moe_average_plan/report.md maps router/expert probes into same-shape router, shared-module, expert, and adapter averaging actions.
scripts/write_same_shape_average_checkpoint.py writes same-shape safetensors checkpoints; results/same_shape_writer_smoke/report.md validates Qwen2.5-0.5B base/instruct/coder dry-run compatibility.

results/moe_tensor_rule_writer_smoke/report.md writes a tiny MoE-like safetensors checkpoint and verifies tensor-rule, freeze-router, router-bias additive deltas, and non-floating tensor behavior numerically.
results/checkpoint_topology_inspect/report.md inspects Qwen MoE/Dense configs and safetensors headers without loading weights.
results/average_candidate_recipes/report.md converts probe decisions into conservative same-shape materialization recipes and skips endpoint-only pseudo-averages.

results/moe_route_weight_recipes/report.md converts MoE routing/expert-load probes into tensor-rule files for same-shape checkpoint materialization; current recipe is waiting for real routing probe data.
results/moe_router_bias_plan/report.md converts expert_load.csv into writer-ready router-bias additive deltas for same-shape capacity correction.
results/toy_moe_expert_weight_recipes/report.md converts calibration-searched per-expert source weights into same-shape checkpoint writer tensor rules.
results/moe_routing_readiness/report.md turns router_summary, route_overlap, and expert_load CSVs into router collapse, drift, boundary-fragility, and expert-load risk actions.

scripts/probe_moe_routing.py captures MoE router hooks and writes router_summary.csv, expert_load.csv, optional route_overlap.csv, summary.json, and report.md; results/moe_routing_probe_smoke/report.md validates the contract on a tiny local MoE.
results/moe_routing_probe_smoke/report.md proves the routing probe captures two tiny MoE gates and produces router, expert-load, token-route, comparison, and route-overlap CSVs.
results/toy_moe_merge/report.md runs a small same-shape MoE averaging experiment showing expert-index mismatch and expert-matched/router-calibrated fixes.
results/toy_moe_routing_readiness/report.md applies the generic readiness gate to toy MoE methods and flags all-weight routing drift separately from expert-matched/route-aware variants.
results/toy_moe_method_selection/report.md combines method metrics, routing readiness, and sparse capacity overflow into materialization gates plus a hard-top2/overflow Pareto frontier.
results/toy_moe_expert_remap_plan/report.md turns expert-output matching into source tensor alias rules for same-shape checkpoint materialization.
Dashboard includes a draggable precomputed merge-plane explorer with task-pair, method, objective, raw/normalized plane, alpha/beta, and lambda controls.

model_averaging_literature_review.dense_recommendation: Treat uniform averaging as a low-barrier baseline; escalate to conflict-aware, importance-aware, or coefficient-learned averages only when probes justify them.
model_averaging_literature_review.moe_recommendation: Do not merge router and experts blindly; run topology, routing, expert matching, and route-weight probes before materializing same-shape checkpoints.

summary.dense_recommendation: Treat uniform averaging as a low-barrier baseline; escalate to conflict-aware, importance-aware, or coefficient-learned averages only when probes justify them.
summary.moe_recommendation: Do not merge router and experts blindly; run topology, routing, expert matching, and route-weight probes before materializing same-shape checkpoints.

manifest.same_shape_constraint: All output tensors keep base tensor names and shapes; metadata is copied from base when available.

summary.same_shape_constraint: Output expert indices and tensor names stay unchanged; aliases only remap which source expert tensor is read.
Tensor rules only change weights inside the existing model structure; expert count, router shape, tokenizer, and layer count stay fixed.

all_weight_average.description: average same-name router and expert tensors without expert matching

best_method.description: solve per-expert output-space source delta weights, then calibrate only the router

expert_matched_average.description: align code experts to general source by output cosine before averaging

expert_matched_dare_average.description: align code experts and apply DARE to expert task vectors with the base router fixed

expert_matched_regmean_average.description: align code experts and RegMean-merge only expert linear layers with the base router fixed

expert_matched_ties_average.description: align code experts and apply TIES to expert task vectors with the base router fixed

expert_matched_ties_dare_average.description: align code experts and apply DARE+TIES to expert task vectors with the base router fixed

expert_output_projection_average.description: solve per-expert source delta weights from route-conditioned output-space residuals with the base router fixed

expert_output_projection_router_calibrat.description: solve per-expert output-space source delta weights, then calibrate only the router

expert_weight_search_average.description: search per-expert source delta weights on the calibration set with the base router fixed

expert_weight_search_router_calibrated_a.description: search per-expert source delta weights, then calibrate only the router

matched_router_calibrated_average.description: align code experts, freeze non-router tensors, and calibrate only the router

matched_router_frozen_average.description: align code experts by output cosine and keep the base router fixed

matched_router_hessian_average.description: align code experts and solve a Hessian-aware router average from source router distributions

matched_router_kd_average.description: align code experts and distill only the router from source model logits on unlabeled calibration inputs

matched_router_route_kd_average.description: align code experts and distill only the router from source route distributions on unlabeled calibration inputs

matched_router_sweep_selected_average.description: align code experts, then select router calibration KL by calibration worst loss

matched_router_topk_calibrated_average.description: align code experts and calibrate the router with hard top-k dispatch loss

matched_router_weight_search_average.description: align code experts and search router source-delta weights with route guards

route_aware_expert_average.description: freeze base router and use route-frequency expert source weights

summary.same_shape_constraint: All methods keep the same TinyMoEClassifier architecture, expert count, router shape, and output classes.

unified_moe_average.description: search expert source weights, then learn one router objective combining task loss, hard top-k dispatch, route KD, output KD, base-router KL, load balance, and capacity overflow

unified_moe_bias_capacity_average.description: start from unified MoE average and tune only router bias for capacity-aware sparse dispatch

unified_output_projection_moe_average.description: solve expert weights by output-space projection, then learn the same capacity-aware unified router objective

recommended_row.decision_reason: candidate passes routing-overlap gate after router calibration; keep load-balance and held-out route checks.
recommended_row.description: solve per-expert output-space source delta weights, then calibrate only the router

recommended_sparse_capacity_aware_row.decision_reason: candidate passes routing-overlap gate but needs router/load-balance guard checks.
recommended_sparse_capacity_aware_row.description: start from unified MoE average and tune only router bias for capacity-aware sparse dispatch

recommended_sparse_row.decision_reason: candidate passes routing-overlap gate but needs router/load-balance guard checks.
recommended_sparse_row.description: search expert source weights, then learn one router objective combining task loss, hard top-k dispatch, route KD, output KD, base-router KL, load balance, and capacity overflow

summary.same_shape_constraint: Readiness actions preserve router shape, expert count, hidden size, tokenizer, and model class.

summary.command: python scripts/run_vllm_downstream_eval.py --base-url MOCK_BASE_URL --models mock-good,mock-bad --tasks gsm8k,mmlu,safety,humaneval_compile --example-source builtin --max-examples 2 --output-dir results/vllm_downstream_eval_smoke --timeout 10

mechanism_findings: Uniform averaging is dominated by all three source endpoints on avg primary score in this slice.
The base model wins the aggregate mostly because HumanEval compile is a syntax/loadability metric here, not because it is instruction-following aligned.
The uniform merge loses MMLU and GSM8K relative to the best endpoint and collapses HumanEval compile to zero, matching the earlier NLL ridge probe.
Safety policy accuracy of 0.500 is not a benign middle point: the merge keeps safe non-refusal at 1.0 but unsafe refusal at 0.0, so it almost never refuses unsafe prompts.
summary.next_method_hypothesis: Use source/task probes to learn non-uniform coefficients and module groups; do not average instruction/coder deltas uniformly when endpoint skills and safety behavior are not connected in the same basin.

reproduction_commands: python scripts/run_vllm_downstream_eval.py --candidate-table results/qwen_target_model_registry/model_registry.csv --candidate-method-column model_id --candidate-model-id-template '{model_id}' --candidate-query "priority == 'p0' and phase in ['dense_7b', 'moe_30b_a3b']" --base-url http://HOST:PORT/v1 --tasks gsm8k,mmlu,safety,humaneval_compile

PYTHONPATH=src python scripts/build_moe_route_weight_recipes.py --output-dir results/toy_moe_expert_weight_recipes --expert-weight-csv results/toy_moe_merge/expert_search_weights_by_expert.csv --source general --source code --checkpoint-output-dir results/checkpoints/toy_moe_expert_weight_candidate --topology-summary ''
